title: [track_10min_16mb] LoRA TTT + SOTA Int5/Int6 (10L, BigramHash, SWA) — Atharva Date (ADIITJ)
body:
## Summary

Document-isolated **LoRA Test-Time Training** at evaluation on top of the SOTA training stack (thwu1, 1.1428 bpb). Zero artifact cost: LoRA adapters are initialized fresh at eval time and discarded after each document — no weights stored in the 16MB artifact.

- **Author:** Atharva Date (ADIITJ)
- **Status:** Non-record (pending 3-seed H100 validation)
- **Expected bpb:** 1.137 – 1.140 (projected)
- **Base:** SOTA thwu1 (1.1428 bpb)

## Key changes from SOTA

| Component | This submission | SOTA (thwu1) |
|---|---|---|
| Training | identical | — |
| Quantization | Int5-MLP + Int6-Attn + zstd-22 | same |
| `swa_start_frac` | **0.35** | 0.40 |
| `warmdown_iters` | **3500** | 3000 |
| Final eval | **Document-isolated LoRA TTT** | Sliding window |

## LoRA TTT design

- Rank-8 adapters on Q and V projections in all 10 attention layers
- Chunk-level TTT: **score chunk first, then train LoRA on it** (no leakage)
- Adam optimizer (β₁=0.9, β₂=0.95, ε=1e-10), lr=0.01
- Adapters reset between documents; optimizer state reset between batches
- 2048-token context window, 256-token chunks, batch of 32 documents

## Artifact budget

Same quantization as SOTA: ~14.3MB (LoRA adds 0 bytes).

## Compliance

- [x] Artifact ≤ 16,000,000 bytes (~14.3MB estimated)
- [x] No network calls during evaluation
- [x] No training data access during evaluation
- [x] TTT uses only already-scored validation tokens
- [x] Training ≤ 10 minutes on 8xH100
- [x] Evaluation ≤ 10 minutes on 8xH100
- [ ] Statistical significance (3 seeds not yet run — **non-record**)

## Attribution

Training architecture from thwu1 (2026-03-20) building on Raahil Shah's PR #162. LoRA TTT design adapted from samacqua (2026-03-17).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body: `_find_docs` splits documents at each BOS but excludes the BOS token of the *next* document from scoring/training. This means most BOS tokens are never evaluated (unlike the standard/ sliding-window eval which scores every token except the first), so the reported `val_bpb` will not be comparable and can be artificially improved. Adjust document slicing so every token in the validation stream (except the very first) is scored exactly once (e.g., include the next document’s BOS in the previous document, as done in earlier LoRA-TTT implementations), and ensure any pre-BOS prefix tokens are handled consistently.
